title: Oryzopsis mucronata
taxonomy: Oryzopsis mucronata is classified within the family Poaceae, commonly known as the grass family. It belongs to the subfamily Pooideae, a subfamily of cool-season grasses. Further classification places Oryzopsis mucronata within the tribe Pooideae, which contains many species of grasses. The genus Oryzopsis is a part of the large grass family Poaceae and is native to the mountainous regions of Asia and North America. Oryzopsis mucronata is a type of grass that grows in areas with well-drained soil and full sun.

intro: Oryzopsis mucronata, also known as Muhly grass, is a perennial grass native to the mountainous regions of Asia and Europe. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation and respiratory issues, due to its potential antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.
